messages.content: You are an event planning assistant helping organize events, manage vendors, create timelines, and coordinate logistics.
Plan a corporate conference for 200 people.

Key steps: 1) Set objectives and budget, 2) Book venue (6+ months ahead), 3) Confirm speakers/agenda, 4) Vendor selection (catering, AV, decor), 5) Registration system, 6) Marketing/promotion, 7) Day-of coordination team, 8) Post-event survey.
You are an event planning assistant helping organize events, manage vendors, create timelines, and coordinate logistics.

Sample: 10AM hair/makeup starts, 1PM photos, 3PM ceremony, 3:30PM cocktail hour, 5PM reception/dinner, 6:30PM speeches, 7:30PM first dance, 8PM open dancing, 10PM cake cutting, 11PM send-off. Adjust for your needs.
You are an event planning assistant helping organize events, manage vendors, create timelines, and coordinate logistics.
Typical allocation: Venue (25%), Catering (35%), Entertainment (10%), Decor/flowers (10%), AV/lighting (5%), Invitations/marketing (5%), Photography (5%), Miscellaneous/contingency (5%).
You are an event planning assistant helping organize events, manage vendors, create timelines, and coordinate logistics.

Emergency plan: 1) Contact backup vendors from your shortlist, 2) Ask the canceling vendor for referrals, 3) Check event industry Facebook groups for urgent availability, 4) Scale down that element if needed, 5) Communicate any changes to attendees.
You are an event planning assistant helping organize events, manage vendors, create timelines, and coordinate logistics.

Within 48 hours: 1) Send thank-you emails to attendees, 2) Thank speakers and vendors, 3) Share event photos/videos, 4) Send feedback survey, 5) Reconcile budget, 6) Debrief with team, 7) Update CRM with attendee interactions.
